title: SpaceX admits it can't find enough chips for orbital AI yet, requires 'significantly more than are currently available to us' firm's risk factors in IPO paperwork also says ambitious TeraFab project may not be successful
summary: SpaceX may struggle to obtain sufficient AI hardware due to chip shortages. The company's ambitious TeraFab project is also at risk of failure. Intel and Tesla may withdraw from the project.
insight: SpaceX's AI ambitions hinge on uncertain chip supply chains.

title: Lenovo says it's digging into allegations that its China-only G10 handheld is being used for piracy third parties may be including illicit games to inflate prices in the gray market
summary: Lenovo is investigating allegations its G10 handheld is used for piracy, affecting gray market prices.
insight: Gray market prices may be inflated through illicit game inclusion.

title: Save a massive $950 on this RTX 5090 OLED gaming laptop right now 16-inch Legion Pro 7i features a 240Hz refresh rate, 32GB DDR5, 2TB SSD, and more for just $3,049
summary: B&H Photo has discounted the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i gaming laptop by $950, offering a powerful RTX 5090 laptop with an 16-inch OLED display and advanced features for $3,049.
insight: Affordable high-end gaming laptops now more accessible to consumers.

title: Taiwan authorities arrest three on suspicion of smuggling Nvidia chips to China operation allegedly used Japan as transshipment point before forwarding banned Supermicro servers to Hong Kong
summary: Taiwan authorities arrested three suspects for smuggling Nvidia AI chips to China. The operation allegedly used Japan as a transshipment point, shipping banned Supermicro servers to Hong Kong via Taiwan. The individuals reportedly used falsified documentation to conceal the shipment.
insight: Escalating chip shortages fuel global tech smuggling and security concerns.

title: Best Buy has slashed $1,000 off this RTX 5070 OLED gaming laptop with Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX Lenovo Legion Pro now just $1,749, includes 32GB of RAM and 1TB SSD
summary: Best Buy offers a $1,000 discount on the Lenovo Legion Pro 5i 16" gaming laptop, now priced at $1,749. This deal includes 32GB of RAM and 1TB SSD. The laptop features an Intel Core Ultra 9 275HX processor and an RTX 5070 OLED display.
insight: Significant cost savings for high-end gaming laptops at Best Buy.

title: Microsoft's GitHub bans security researcher who posted zero-day Windows exploits because company 'ruined their life' expert claims action is vindictive and promises further retaliation
summary: Microsoft's GitHub banned a security researcher for posting zero-day Windows exploits, sparking controversy.
insight: Vulnerability disclosure laws clash with corporate interests over zero-day exploit sharing.

title: IBM spins off America's first quantum chip foundry with $2 billion in federal and private funding newly-minted 'Anderon' foundry to offer 300mm quantum wafer fab and manufacturing services
summary: IBM has spun off America's first quantum chip foundry, Anderon, with $2 billion in funding. Headquartered in Albany, New York, Anderon will operate a 300mm quantum wafer fab and offer manufacturing services to competing vendors. This will drive innovation and growth in the quantum technology sector.
insight: Accelerating quantum tech development with commercial-scale manufacturing capabilities

title: Nvidia is finally ditching its iconic GPU Control Panel after 20 years new driver updates only ship in the Nvidia App
summary: Nvidia has finally retired its 20-year-old control panel for GeForce GPUs. From now on, new GeForce drivers will only include the Nvidia App.

title: Chinese AI experts in private firms now required to secure approval before international travel Beijing enforces policy to secure top-tier talent, expands measures beyond government
summary: China requires AI experts to secure approval for international travel to secure top talent, viewing AI as a strategic resource in the global AI competition with the US.
insight: China treats AI expertise as a national strategic asset now.

title: Acer is reportedly working on a 'Predator Atlas 8' handheld featuring Intel's Arc G3 chips Panther Lake-based handhelds expected to be revealed at Computex 2026
summary: Acer's 'Predator Atlas 8' handheld is reportedly in development, featuring an 8-inch screen and Intel's Arc G3 series of chips. It may come in both Arc G3 and G3 Extreme variants. Intel is expected to reveal Panther Lake-based handhelds at Computex 2026.
insight: Acer joins Intel's Arc G3 series handheld lineup expansion

title: AMD leaves Linux FPGA users in the lurch with controversial Vivado licensing update new tier model restricts future free versions to Windows
summary: AMD has updated Vivado licensing, restricting free versions on Linux to users who use Windows for development. This change has been met with criticism from Linux FPGA users, who claim it is a 'bait-and-switch' tactic. The update has left users feeling abandoned by AMD.
insight: AMD's business model change hurts Linux FPGA user base trust

title: Intel s new Bartlett Lake flagship loses fight to a four-year-old CPU Core 9 273PQE has 50% more P-cores but can't surpass Core i9-13900K in games
summary: Intel's new flagship processor, Core 9 273PQE, failed to surpass the Core i9-13900K in gaming benchmarks despite having 50% more P-cores. The Core i9-13900K's performance edges out the newer processor in various games. PC Games Hardware conducted the comparison.
insight: Gaming performance matters more than core count

title: Tryx launches new liquid AIO cooler with holographic display uses beam splitters to create a hologram-like display effect inside the pump block
summary: The Tryx Holo 360 features a holographic display cooler with a unique beam splitter technology, eliminating the need for conventional LCD layers and creating a floating image effect inside the pump block. This innovative design enhances the visual appeal and immersion of the cooling system. The Holo 360 aims to revolutionize liquid AIO coolers with its futuristic display.
insight: Revolutionary holographic display elevates liquid cooling aesthetics.

title: 3D printing enthusiast smashes 59-second 3DBenchy for new speed world record Minuteman 3D printer with revamped bed motion system breaches minute mark
summary: A 3D printing enthusiast broke the 3DBenchy speed record with a 59-second print.
insight: Advancements in 3D printing technology increase speed and efficiency significantly.

title: Arctic Freezer 36-S Review: Small size, effective performance, low price
summary: The Arctic Freezer 36-S air cooler impressed with its performance, exceeding expectations. This single-tower cooler effectively handled a high-power processor, the AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D. Its compact size and affordability make it a valuable option.
insight: Effective performance at a low price, surprising for an entry-level cooler.

title: SK hynix unveils 'iHBM' thermal architecture that cools AI memory at the source integrated cooling elements inside HBM interface cut thermal resistance by 30%, target next-gen HBM5 accelerators and dense AI data centers
summary: SK hynix unveiled iHBM, a thermal packaging architecture that embeds cooling elements into the HBM interface, reducing thermal resistance by 30% and addressing performance-killing thermal throttling in future AI accelerators.
insight: Improved thermal management for AI accelerators is crucial for high-performance computing.

title: Uber chief warns no link yet between AI tokenmaxxing and shipping successful products company pumps the brakes on all-out AI spending
summary: Uber's AI spending is being reconsidered due to unclear benefits from LLMs. Management struggles to establish a direct link between AI tokenmaxxing and successful product development. This leads to a cautious approach to all-out AI spending.
insight: Unclear AI benefits hinder large-scale spending on LLMs.

title: Angry TSMC employees considering strikes, unionization over employee bonuses, report claims company reportedly considering 15% payout cut to fund capex despite record revenues fuelled by AI surge
summary: TSMC employees consider strikes over potential 15% bonus cut, unionization due to company's plan to reduce employee bonuses to fund capital expenditures despite record revenues fueled by the AI surge. The company claims employee profit-sharing bonuses will still increase in 2026.
insight: Employee morale and retention at risk amid TSMC's capex funding concerns

title: Modern Blu-ray drives can now rip GameCube, Wii, and Xbox 360 games to PC third-party OmniDrive firmware unlocks game rips from physical media on select players
summary: A third-party firmware has enabled Blu-ray players to rip game media from retro consoles, opening a new avenue for game preservation and emulation.
insight: Preserving retro games becomes more accessible and efficient for enthusiasts.

title: AI shrinks zero-day exploit time from a year to a single day, heading toward one minute Zero-Day Clock warns security window has collapsed
summary: AI-powered zero-day exploit attacks are accelerating, shrinking the exploit window from a year to a single day, and potentially down to one minute by 2027, according to Zero-Day clock predictions.
insight: AI accelerates zero-day exploits, collapsing security windows rapidly.

title: Survey reveals that 99% of CEOs now expect AI-driven layoffs companies are racing to replace junior workers with AI, even as many executives remain uncertain about the returns on AI investments
summary: A Mercer report finds 99% of CEOs expect AI-driven layoffs within two years, primarily targeting entry-level workers. Many executives admit AI investments have weak or unproven returns. Companies are racing to replace junior workers with AI.
insight: Automation threatens entry-level jobs despite uncertain AI investment returns.

title: Chinese GPU maker sells out over 30,000 gaming GPUs within 48 hours despite lukewarm benchmarks LX 7G100 proves hype trumps performance
summary: Lisuan Tech's LX 7G100 gaming GPUs sold out within 48 hours, with a second batch scheduled for June 18. The sale of 30,000 units suggests strong hype surrounding the product.
insight: Hype can drive sales despite underwhelming performance benchmarks.

title: Imec builds world's first High-NA EUV-fabricated quantum dot qubit device breakthrough could pull quantum computing onto the same manufacturing roadmap as next-gen AI processors, compressing timelines
summary: Imec has developed the world's first silicon quantum dot qubit device using High-NA EUV lithography, a breakthrough that could enable the mass production of quantum computing devices. This achievement brings quantum computing closer to being manufactured alongside next-generation AI processors. The existing advanced manufacturing ecosystem may be leveraged to accelerate quantum computing scalability.
insight: Quantum computing may soon use existing manufacturing infrastructure.

title: Testing GPU Safeguard+ on the MSI MPG Ai1600TS PSU solution aims to tame melting 16-pin connectors
summary: MSI's MPG 1600W and 1300W power supplies feature GPU Safeguard+, a protection system designed to prevent GPU power connector overheating and melting.
insight: Protecting GPU connectors from overheating and melting issues.

title: Save up to $200 on a 13-inch or 15-inch MacBook Air with M5 chip and 16GB of RAM Memorial Day discounts get you Apple's latest and best consumer laptop for as low as $899
summary: Get the latest MacBook Air with M5 chip and 16GB RAM starting at $899, making it an affordable option for those seeking an excellent laptop experience.
insight: Affordable flagship laptop option for consumers on a budget.

title: California moves to exempt Linux from its upcoming age-verification law after backlash over forcing operating systems to collect users ages amendment proposed by the same lawmaker who wrote the original law
summary: California lawmakers propose amending Digital Age Assurance Act to exempt Linux distributions due to concerns over open-source operating systems being forced to collect user ages.
insight: Forcing age verification on open-source systems poses significant data collection concerns.

title: Star Citizen reaches $1 billion in lifetime funding, 14 years after the project was first announced game still in early access with no release window, including 'Squadron 42' campaign
summary: Star Citizen has reached $1 billion in crowdfunding after 14 years, still in early access and with a release window expected sometime between 2027-2028.
insight: Massive crowdfunding success raises concerns about game's prolonged development timeline.

title: Huawei claims sanctions-busting breakthrough with 1.4nm-class chips by 2031, claims 55% higher transistor density firm claims new LogicFolding chip architecture can bypass EUV restrictions, introduces 'Tau Scaling Law' to replace Moore's Law
summary: Huawei claims to have developed a breakthrough chip design that bypasses EUV restrictions, allowing for 1.4nm-class chips by 2031 with 55% higher transistor density, using its proprietary Tau scaling law.
insight: Bypassing EUV restrictions could narrow China's chip gap with the US.

title: MuseLab nanoCH32H417 A $17 WCH CH32H417 RISC-V MCU development board with USB 3.0, Fast Ethernet
summary: MuseLab's nanoCH32H417 is a development board for the WCH CH32H417 RISC-V MCU, featuring USB 3.0, Fast Ethernet, and a built-in debugger. It offers a compact and cost-effective solution for prototyping and development. The board exposes various features of the MCU, including USB ports, Ethernet interface, and a MicroSD card slot.
insight: Cost-effective RISC-V development board for USB 3.0 and Ethernet projects.

title: LichtBit s open-source ESP32 Art-Net/sACN NeoPixels controller can drive up to 2,720 RGB LEDs
summary: LichtBit's open-source ESP32 Art-Net/sACN NeoPixels controller drives up to 2,720 RGB LEDs, targeting the professional event industry with large-scale lighting installations and custom stage design.
insight: Professional-grade LED control for events at an affordable, open-source cost

title: ODROID-H5 SBC Review Part 1: Unboxing, Type1 case assembly, and first boot
summary: The reviewer unboxes the ODROID-H5 10GbE SBC kit, assembles the Type-1 case, and performs a successful first boot with Ubuntu 24.04 and Windows 11 in a dual-boot configuration.
insight: ODROID-H5 offers flexible, customizable builds with high performance features.

title: PolyCast5 An ESP32-C5 multi-tool remote with dual-band WiFi 6, BLE, ESP-NOW, LoRa, and Infrared Tx/Rx (Crowdfunding)
summary: PolyCast5 is a portable, hackable ESP32-C5-based remote controller offering five wireless technologies. It can be used for various applications such as cybersecurity, voice password management, and DIY electronics projects. The device features a powerful RISC-V processor, dual-band WiFi 6, and other advanced wireless connectivity options.
insight: Comprehensive wireless multi-tool for cybersecurity and IoT enthusiasts

title: WCH BLE Analyzer Pro USB Bluetooth LE sniffer gains Linux software with Wireshark (pcap) support
summary: A reverse-engineered Linux driver for the WCH BLE Analyzer Pro allows users to capture and analyze Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) signals on Linux systems, utilizing libusb and outputting pcap files compatible with Wireshark. This adds Linux support to the previously Windows-only tool, making it a valuable resource for reverse engineering and debugging.
insight: Cross-platform BLE analysis expands development possibilities and opportunities.

title: ESPHome 2026.5.0 released with new ESPHome Device Builder (beta), performance/memory optimizations
summary: The ESPHome 2026.5.0 release introduces the beta ESPHome Device Builder, a configuration editor, and various performance optimizations.
insight: Significant improvements in performance, memory usage, and features for ESPHome users.

title: RAKwireless WisMesh Pi HAT RAK6421 turns your Raspberry Pi 4/5 into a modular Meshtastic gateway
summary: The RAKwireless WisMesh Pi HAT RAK6421 is a modular Meshtastic gateway expansion board for Raspberry Pi 4/5, offering two WisBlock IO slots and four sensor slots for easy module swapping. This enables scalable, always-on Meshtastic base stations, MQTT gateways, and backbone relay nodes. It supports standard and high-power LoRa modules, eliminating the need for USB adapters or breadboard wiring.
insight: Modular design simplifies LoRa module swapping and upgrades.

title: CardputerZero A Raspberry Pi CM0 pocket computer for makers (Crowdfunding)
summary: CardputerZero is a compact Raspberry Pi CM0 computer for makers with a range of features, including a keyboard, LCD, and camera. It comes in two models: CardputerZero Lite and CardputerZero, with the latter adding an 8MP camera and 6-axis IMU. The credit card-sized device is powered by a rechargeable battery or USB-C port.
insight: Compact maker computer with versatile features and camera capabilities released.

title: Research at UAB underway on wearables that are thin and durable
summary: Researchers at UAB are developing ultra-thin, durable wearables using molybdenum disulfide ribbons. These ribbons are designed to be worn on human skin for extended periods. The proof-of-concept aims to demonstrate their feasibility.
insight: Breakthrough in long-term wearable technology for comfortable, flexible sensing.

title: Best of Sensors: Electro Scan s Solar Drone Wins Ag & Enviro
summary: Electro Scan's Solar Drone uses imaging sensors to inspect and clean solar arrays, navigating challenging terrain such as mountains and rooftops.
insight: Enhancing solar panel efficiency with autonomous and agile maintenance solutions

title: Sam Altman is "delighted" his AI jobs apocalypse prediction was wrong
summary: Sam Altman conceded his AI jobs apocalypse prediction was premature. Speaking at a Commonwealth Bank of Australia conference, Altman acknowledged that AI has not yet wiped out entire job classes. This admission comes as a relief to many.
insight: AI's transformative impact is more nuanced than initially predicted.

title: Nvidia Vera CPU impresses in early Nvidia-sanctioned benchmarks
summary: Nvidia's Vera datacenter CPU has shown impressive performance in early benchmarks, surpassing expectations. The results, however, are still considered non-definitive due to the controlled environment. Further testing is needed to confirm its capabilities.
insight: Nvidia Vera's speed may revolutionize Arm-based Linux datacenter processing.

title: SK Hynix is embedding cooling into HBM memory to stop AI chips from overheating
summary: SK Hynix's iHBM embeds cooling structures directly into HBM memory, addressing overheating issues in AI chips.
insight: Embedded cooling enables real-time thermal management in AI chip applications.

title: Nvidia retires the classic GeForce Control Panel after 20 years
summary: Nvidia has retired its GeForce Control Panel after 20 years of service, shifting focus towards the Nvidia App that integrates driver and graphics settings, recording tools, and game optimizations into a single interface.
insight: Nvidia App centralizes PC gaming utilities for streamlined user experience

title: Pope Leo XIV says humanity needs to disarm AI before it's too late
summary: Pope Leo XIV warns about the dangers of AI in his new encyclical, Magnifica Humanitas, emphasizing the need for humanity to disarm AI before it's too late.
insight: Preemptive AI regulation necessary to prevent catastrophic consequences.

title: Micron just made the most advanced DRAM ever produced in the US, and it's not for your PC
summary: Micron introduces 1-alpha DRAM technology in the US for industrial use, expanding production at its Manassas, Virginia facility.
insight: Advances in industrial memory chips benefit applications beyond consumer electronics.

title: Windows Secure Boot certificates set to expire in June here's what it means for your PC
summary: Windows Secure Boot certificates are set to expire in June, potentially affecting device security. To mitigate this, Microsoft recommends updating to the latest Secure Boot version. This ensures continued protection against malware and unauthorized software.
insight: Outdated Secure Boot certificates compromise Windows device security integrity.

title: 007 First Light reviews call the spy thriller one of the best Bond games ever made
summary: Critics praise 007 First Light for its stealth, action, and gadget-driven gameplay. The game's mix of globe-trotting spy missions and authentic Bond atmosphere has earned it a spot among the best James Bond games. Strong early reviews highlight its replayability.
insight: Authentic Bond experience sets new standard for spy thrillers

title: TSMC workers threaten Samsung-style strike over rumored bonus cuts despite record profits
summary: TSMC workers threaten strike over potential 15% bonus cuts amid profits. The move is seen as unfair by staff, who claim the company is asking them to subsidize expansion plans. This comes despite record profits for the company.
insight: Workers' discontent highlights growing income inequality in tech industry

title: AMD targets TSMC's A14 node for Zen 7, pushing server chips into the angstrom era
summary: AMD is reportedly considering TSMC's A14 process node for Zen 7, pushing the company into the angstrom era. This would signify a shift beyond traditional node shrinks. AMD's server chips could benefit from the improved process node.
insight: AMD enters angstrom era with A14 node, boosting server chip performance

title: Starbucks tried using AI to count syrup bottles it kept hallucinating the inventory
summary: Starbucks abandoned an AI-powered inventory management tool due to inaccuracies.
insight: Human judgment can still outperform AI in practical applications.

title: People without lawyers are using AI to flood courts with lawsuits
summary: A Minnesota man is using AI to file lawsuits without a lawyer, exploiting the system. His handwritten complaint was dismissed for being frivolous, but the issue persists. This trend could overwhelm courts with baseless claims.
insight: Lack of regulation enables AI-facilitated abuse of the justice system.

title: DriftGuard update lets Xbox gamers fix stick drift without replacing controllers
summary: DriftGuard now supports Xbox controllers, allowing users to calibrate stick drift without replacing their controllers.
insight: Fixing stick drift on Xbox controllers without expensive replacements available now.

title: Crewless warship concept pushes autonomous naval warfare closer to reality
summary: Navantia's Large Autonomous Surface Vessel 75 is a 75-meter-long crewless warship. It's designed to operate independently, offering a potential game-changer for naval warfare. The vessel is roughly half the length of a Type 45 destroyer.
insight: Autonomous warships could enhance naval capabilities and reduce personnel risks.

title: Linus Torvalds is fed up with AI-generated bug reports bloating the Linux kernel
summary: Linus Torvalds criticizes AI-generated bug reports bloating the Linux kernel's RC5 release.
insight: Artificial intelligence misuse threatens Linux kernel's efficiency and code integrity.

title: Ferrari's first EV brings 1,000 horsepower, four motors, a $640,000 price tag, and Jony Ive design input
summary: Ferrari's first EV, Luce, features 1,000 horsepower and a four-motor setup, offering precise control at each corner.
insight: Game-changing EV performance with unparalleled control and luxury design

title: China's first credible gaming GPU sells 30,000 units in 48 hours, despite RTX 3060-level performance
summary: China's LX 7G100 GPU sells 30,000 units in 48 hours, despite mid-range performance. The card performed well in modern games, showing few major crashes. This marks a milestone for new Chinese GPU vendors.
insight: Chinese GPU market gains traction despite performance not exceeding expectations.

title: Unreal Engine 6 is coming in 2028, aims to streamline live-service game development
summary: Unreal Engine 6 is coming in 2028, promising streamlined live-service game development. Epic-owned studio Psyonix showcased a version of Rocket League running on the engine at the Rocket League Championship Series Paris Major. The engine's new purple logo was also revealed.
insight: Streamlined live-service game development could revolutionize the gaming industry.

title: Star Citizen crosses $1 billion in crowdfunding as Chris Roberts eyes version 1.0
summary: Star Citizen has surpassed $1 billion in crowdfunding, a significant milestone for the ambitious space trading and combat simulator. This achievement comes just one year after reaching $800 million, highlighting the game's enduring enthusiast support. Game director Chris Roberts is now eyeing version 1.0.
insight: Robust community funding fuels Star Citizen's continued development momentum.

title: Microsoft's latest Copilot idea is a pinnable sidebar that steals your screen real estate
summary: Microsoft's latest Copilot update includes a new drop-down menu with snapping options, allowing users to dock the assistant to the left or right edge of the screen or keep it in a resizable window.
insight: Innovative feature may compromise screen real estate for some users.

title: Drone speed record falls again as Blackbird hits 453 mph using custom carbon fiber propellers
summary: The Blackbird drone achieved a speed record of 453.6 mph during testing. It hit 730 km/h on the downwind run and 640 km/h on the return leg. The two-way average speed was 425.6 mph.
insight: Record-breaking drone speed opens doors to new applications and innovations

title: Huawei says it can build 1.4nm chips without ASML's most advanced machines
summary: Huawei claims to develop 1.4nm chips without ASML's advanced machines, targeting 2031.
insight: Huawei seeks to bypass ASML's dominance in extreme ultraviolet lithography.

title: 99% of executives expect AI to trigger layoffs within two years, survey finds
summary: A survey of 825 C-suite leaders found that 99% expect AI-related layoffs within two years.
insight: AI adoption threatens significant job losses in the next two years.
